Left panel: a teetering town of impossible towers, scaffolds, and arches built on cracked, splitting earth; a large shaggy beast wanders empty doorways while small hooded attendants flee a pale segmented worm across tilled fields. Center panel: on a curtained theatrical stage, two armored half-mechanical knights freeze mid-strike with crossed swords, weapons raised but halted; masked attendants in robes tug a saffron thread from the wings while a workshop of gears and a great spindle of golden thread sits beneath the stage. Right panel: a towering loom and ruined mill rise toward a black solar eclipse; tiny figures climb ladders of thread toward a rocket-shaped reliquary as golden coins spill down a ramp into a sheltered workshop below, where small figures are sorted inside and outside its walls. A single unspooling saffron thread runs continuously across all three panels, binding them together.

alt:
An intricate panoramic triptych painting in the style of Hieronymus Bosch, rendered in oil on oak panels under a cold, dark sky with swirling aurora-like veils of pale cyan and plum.
In the left panel, tiny field workers and gardeners navigate a strange, organic stone structure resembling a hollow shell with pulsing red veins. On one side, healthy cattle graze in lush green pastures, while on the other side of a dividing wall, empty stone paths lead into dark tunnels. Gardeners measure golden cooking oil into standardized rectangular stone blocks.
In the center panel, a wide, dark river or canal cuts through the landscape. Tiny sailors on wooden vessels dredge old gold coins from the plum-colored waters, using them to construct a sprawling, organic stone bridge. In the background, a theatrical procession of actors in oversized, expressive wooden masks marches across a winding, multi-tiered wooden stage under heavy curtains.
In the right panel, a dark, soot-stained region where tiny figures carry heavy stones into underground tunnels. These tunnels visibly loop beneath the earth and connect back to the left panel's entrance. Families watch from hollow chambers carved into the rock, looking up at the cold, starry sky.
